feedback.details: mmdc validation exits 0 successfully (line 6-79). Syntax uses proper C4Container conventions with escaped HTML entities (`#lt;br#gt;`) for line breaks. All 40+ relationships (lines 45-78) parse correctly. No rendering errors or syntax anomalies detected.

All required containers present with proper labels: API Gateway (line 9, OpenAI/Anthropic-compatible), Orchestrator Service (line 17, complexity classification), Model Router (line 18, load balancing/circuit breaking), Local Model Instances via Qwen/Nemotron adapters (lines 20-24), Frontier API Adapter (line 25), Monitoring/Prometheus exporter (line 27), Health Check Service (line 26), Administrative Interface (line 29), Persistent Storage via Consul/Vault integrations (lines 31-32). Technology stacks, responsibilities, and replication factors ('2x') properly labeled throughout.

Complete data flow: API Gateway → Orchestrator (line 45) → Model Router (line 46) → Local/Frontier Models (lines 47-49). Asynchronous flows correctly use dashed lines: Model Router → Langfuse trace collector (line 54), Langfuse → Offline Self-Learning (line 55), Self-Learning → Model Router updates (line 56), Telemetry pulls (lines 57-60), Dead letter queue path (line 74). Bidirectional health checks shown via BiRel (line 73). Synchronous flows use solid lines.

Full PRD alignment demonstrated: Nomad deployment topology with node pool constraints (lines 14, 20-24, 27-33), Consul/Vault integration containers (lines 31-32) with API connections (lines 66-67), Golang implementation labels throughout (lines 9, 17-24), bifrost usage for message routing (lines 9, 45, 17-21), hardware telemetry with VRAM-aware routing (line 17, line 46), network segmentation between DMZ (line 8), Firewall (line 11), and Internal VPC (line 12).

Narrative (lines 2-3) contains exactly 200 words, explicitly stated at end. Word count falls within 150-250 threshold. Covers container purposes, bifrost messaging, mTLS encryption, hardware telemetry integration, developer journey ('zero-code-change integration'), operations journey ('observe self-learning reports'), and deployment topology. All required aspects addressed concisely.

Horizontal scaling demonstrated via load balancers (Qwen LB line 19, Nemotron LB line 22) connecting to pools of identical adapters (lines 20-21, 23-24). Configuration artifacts separated from application code in dedicated 'Nomad Configuration' boundary (lines 13-16). Adding new LLM models shown as configuration change: Admin Interface → Nomad Job update (line 70: 'Update Job Spec (add new LLM model)') without modifying application containers.

Complete failure handling visualization: (1) Fallback paths explicit between Qwen↔Nemotron (lines 76-77: 'Fallback Request on [X] failure'). (2) Circuit breaker patterns marked on Model Router relationships (lines 47-49, 76-77). (3) <500ms timeout thresholds shown on API Gateway (line 45), Model Router (lines 47-49, 76-77), and load balancers. (4) Dead letter queue path after 3 retries (line 74, container at line 33). All requirements met.

Security zones explicit: DMZ for API Gateway (line 8), Firewall boundary (line 11, dashed), Internal VPC (line 12). mTLS shown on all internal service flows (lines 45-49, 61-73) and bifrost connection (line 45). Cost differentiation visible: 'Low Cost' for local models (lines 47-48, 76-77) vs 'High Cost' for Frontier (line 49). VRAM utilization tiers shown: Qwen 24GB (line 39), Nemotron 48GB (line 40), with explicit hardware boundaries and adapter memory constraints (lines 20, 22: 32GB).

overall_summary: This C2 Container diagram (Round 6) is production-ready and exceptionally complete, meeting all sprint contract criteria without gaps. The Mermaid syntax validates successfully (mmdc exit 0), all 10+ required C4 Level 2 containers are present with proper technology stacks, replication factors, and responsibilities. Relationship accuracy is excellent with correct distinction between synchronous solid lines and asynchronous dashed lines for the self-learning trace flow. PRD alignment is comprehensive covering Nomad topology, Consul/Vault integration, Golang labels, bifrost usage, and hardware telemetry. The narrative (200 words) falls within the required 150-250 range and covers all required topics. Extensibility is clearly demonstrated with load balancers connecting to adapter pools and Nomad configuration separation. Failure handling is fully visualized with fallback paths, circuit breakers, timeout thresholds, and dead letter queue. Security zones (DMZ/Firewall/Internal VPC), mTLS authentication, cost differentiation, and VRAM-aware hardware boundaries are all explicit. This represents a significant improvement over Rounds 1-4 which failed on failure handling and relationship accuracy, and maintains the quality achieved in Round 5.
